hypothesis: If a concept bottleneck model is trained to learn orthogonal, property-specific embeddings and their weighted sum is added as a steering vector to intermediate activations of a frozen chemistry LLM during generation, then the model will produce molecules whose properties (e.g., QED, LogP) more accurately match specified targets with lower MAE/Best100MAE and higher generative efficiency while maintaining validity, uniqueness, novelty, and synthesizability—even on out-of-distribution targets—than vanilla, ActAdd, LoRA, or full fine-tuning baselines because the learned embeddings disentangle and linearly control property-relevant directions in the model’s representation space.
